title: Getting Started with Flutter
subtitle: Build beautiful cross-platform apps with Google's UI toolkit.
author: John Doe

content.value: Flutter is an open-source UI software development kit created by Google. It is used to develop applications for Android, iOS, Linux, Mac, Windows, Google Fuchsia, and the web from a single codebase.
To start building with Flutter, you need to install the Flutter SDK and set up an editor (like VS Code or Android Studio).

flutter doctor
flutter create my_app
cd my_app
flutter run
Here's a simple 'Hello World' Flutter app:
This code creates a basic app with an app bar and centered text.

Widgets: Everything in Flutter is a widget
State management: Handling app state
Hot reload: Instant updates during development
Pub.dev: Package repository
Flutter makes it easy to create beautiful, high-performance apps for multiple platforms. Start exploring the rich set of widgets and packages available.

meta.title: Flutter Tutorial: Getting Started with Cross-Platform Development
meta.description: Learn Flutter from scratch with this step-by-step guide. Covers installation, first app, and key concepts.

faq.question: Is Flutter only for mobile?
faq.answer: No, Flutter supports mobile, web, desktop, and embedded devices.

faq.question: What language does Flutter use?
faq.answer: Flutter uses Dart, a client-optimized language for fast apps.
